idea.hook: Where does our Solar System *actually* end?
facts: From the Sun to Neptune (our outermost major planet) is about 30 Astronomical Units (AU).
The *inner edge* of the Oort Cloud, where billions of icy objects reside, begins around 2,000 AU from the Sun.
The *outer edge* of the Oort Cloud extends up to a staggering 100,000 AU (over 1.5 light-years) from the Sun.
idea.payoff: The familiar planetary system is just a tiny speck at the very heart of our colossal Solar System!
idea.title: Solar System's Secret Size! 🤯

scenes.text: Where does our Solar System *actually* end?
scenes.visual: Starts zoomed in on a bright Sun, then slightly zooms out to show the orbits of inner planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars). Stars are faintly visible in the dark background.
Continues zooming out. All 8 major planets with their orbits are visible. Neptune's orbit is highlighted with a visual measurement line indicating '30 AU'.

scenes.text: Inner Oort Cloud: starts at ~2,000 AU
scenes.visual: Significant zoom out. The entire planetary system (Sun + planets) is now a tiny, bright dot in the center. A faint, diffuse, spherical 'halo' representing the inner Oort Cloud appears far beyond Neptune's orbit, with a measurement line showing '2,000 AU'.

scenes.text: Outer Oort Cloud: up to ~100,000 AU (1.5 Light-Years!)
scenes.visual: Massive zoom out. The planetary system is almost imperceptible. The inner Oort Cloud is tiny. A much larger, fainter, spherical shell representing the *outer* Oort Cloud fills the screen. Distant stars are visible in the background.

scenes.text: Our familiar planetary system is just a tiny speck!
scenes.visual: Full view of the colossal Oort Cloud sphere, almost filling the entire screen. The central planetary system is a minuscule, nearly invisible dot. The vastness of space and faint distant stars are emphasized.
